title: ophrys apifera var. botteronii
intro: Ophrys apifera var. botteronii, commonly known as the bee orchid, is a rare and endemic variety of orchid found in the Swiss Alps. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The bee orchid is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues and alleviating respiratory problems, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional herbal preparations, the bee orchid is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
